prompt: Explain photosynthesis to a curious adult non-scientist in around 150 words. Avoid a bulleted list.
response: Imagine plants as tiny solar-powered chefs! Photosynthesis is their amazing superpower. They take simple ingredients – sunlight, water from the soil, and a gas called carbon dioxide from the air – and, using a special green pigment called chlorophyll, they cook up their own food: sugar. This sugar gives them the energy to grow, bloom, and even produce fruits and vegetables for us to enjoy. As a bonus, this cooking process also releases the oxygen we breathe, making it essential for almost all life on Earth. So, next time you see a green leaf, remember it's a miniature food factory, working hard to keep our world alive!
